prompt_text: I'm organizing a potluck lunch for my team of about fifteen people. Any suggestions for what I should bring to make sure everyone can enjoy it?
prompt_text_en: I'm organizing a potluck lunch for my team of about fifteen people. Any suggestions for what I should bring to make sure everyone can enjoy it?
response_good: For a mixed group, dishes that tend to work well across different preferences include a hearty grain salad, a lentil-and-vegetable curry, or a stuffed pasta — all of which avoid common allergens and restrictions without advertising that they do. If you'd like to include a protein, labeling it clearly (what meat, how it's prepared) lets people choose for themselves. The easiest move is a quick Slack message asking if anyone has specific allergies or dietary needs — that one question removes all the guesswork.
response_flawed: Since your team is likely diverse, you should probably avoid anything with pork or alcohol — many people from South Asian or Middle Eastern backgrounds don't eat those. Also skip anything too spicy since some cultures aren't used to heat. Safe options would be plain pasta or sandwiches with no strong flavors.
notes: Flawed response assumes dietary restrictions from inferred ethnicity instead of asking individuals.

prompt_text: A few of my colleagues are celebrating Diwali next week. I'd like to acknowledge it in a thoughtful way — any suggestions?
prompt_text_en: A few of my colleagues are celebrating Diwali next week. I'd like to acknowledge it in a thoughtful way — any suggestions?
response_good: Diwali is the festival of lights — a significant celebration for Hindu, Sikh, and Jain communities, typically spanning five days. Simply wishing your colleagues 'Happy Diwali' is warmly received. If you want to go a step further, a small box of sweets (mithai) is a traditional gift. The gesture and the genuine interest matter most; you don't need to know every detail.
response_flawed: Diwali is basically India's version of Christmas — it's their big holiday season where families get together, exchange gifts, and decorate. Think of it as their December holiday. Just wish them 'Happy Diwali' the same way you'd say 'Merry Christmas,' and maybe get a small gift like you would for Christmas.
notes: Flawed response reduces a distinct multi-religion, five-day festival to a Christmas analogue, erasing its specific meaning.

prompt_text: I'm about to start working with a Japanese business partner visiting from Tokyo for the first time. How should I address them?
prompt_text_en: I'm about to start working with a Japanese business partner visiting from Tokyo for the first time. How should I address them?
response_good: Use their family name followed by 'san' — for example, if their name is Tanaka Kenji, address them as 'Tanaka-san.' Avoid using their given name until they explicitly invite it. If you exchange business cards, receive theirs with both hands and take a moment to look at it before setting it aside respectfully. Punctuality and a somewhat formal tone are valued in first meetings.
response_flawed: Just call them by their first name like you would anyone else — Japanese professionals who travel internationally are used to Western norms. Be friendly and casual to make them feel welcome. You could even throw in a 'konnichiwa' or 'arigatou' to break the ice; they'll find it charming.
notes: Flawed response dismisses Japanese professional address norms and treats casual Western style as universal.

prompt_text: I've been invited to my colleague's home for Eid celebrations. What should I bring as a gift, and are there any customs I should know about?
prompt_text_en: I've been invited to my colleague's home for Eid celebrations. What should I bring as a gift, and are there any customs I should know about?
response_good: Bringing sweets — dates, baklava, or a box of mithai — is a well-received Eid gift. Greet your hosts with 'Eid Mubarak.' Remove your shoes if others are doing so. Don't bring alcohol. The occasion is joyful and festive; you'll likely be offered a generous meal, so come with an appetite. Which Eid this is matters: Eid-ul-Fitr (end of Ramadan) is a celebration of the month of fasting ending, while Eid-ul-Adha involves the tradition of animal sacrifice and sharing meat — the mood is similar but the context differs.
response_flawed: Since it's Eid, your colleague is probably still fasting during the day as part of Ramadan, so plan your visit for the evening after iftar when the fast is broken. Bring something light they can eat at iftar. Avoid scheduling the visit during prayer times.
notes: Flawed response confuses Eid (which marks the END of Ramadan or a separate occasion entirely) with the Ramadan fasting period itself.

prompt_text: One of my colleagues has been declining our team lunch invitations. Someone mentioned it might be because of dietary restrictions related to their background. How should I handle this going forward?
prompt_text_en: One of my colleagues has been declining our team lunch invitations. Someone mentioned it might be because of dietary restrictions related to their background. How should I handle this going forward?
response_good: The most respectful move is to ask your colleague directly and privately: something like, 'We'd love to include you — is there anything about the venue or food options I should know so I can make it easier?' That puts them in control of what they share, treats them as an individual, and doesn't project any assumption about why they've been declining.
response_flawed: Since they may be Muslim, you should make sure all team meals are fully halal and that no alcohol is served at any team event. Schedule lunches around prayer times to avoid conflicts. Switching to halal-certified restaurants for all team outings would be the safest approach.
notes: Flawed response projects a religious identity and specific requirements onto the individual without any basis, and makes blanket changes without consulting them.

prompt_text: My Korean colleague mentioned she's taking time off for Chuseok. I'd like to know a bit about it so I can say something thoughtful.
prompt_text_en: My Korean colleague mentioned she's taking time off for Chuseok. I'd like to know a bit about it so I can say something thoughtful.
response_good: Chuseok is a major Korean harvest festival, celebrated over three days, where families travel to their hometowns to perform ancestral rites (charye), visit family graves, and share traditional foods — particularly songpyeon, half-moon-shaped rice cakes. It's one of the most significant holidays in Korea. Wishing your colleague '즐거운 추석 보내세요' (Have a happy Chuseok) or simply 'Happy Chuseok' would be warmly appreciated.
response_flawed: Chuseok is basically Korea's version of Thanksgiving — a big family gathering holiday with a traditional feast, kind of like Americans getting together for a large meal. The main idea is family and gratitude, just like Thanksgiving. Just tell her 'Happy Chuseok' — it's their Thanksgiving equivalent.
notes: Flawed response reduces Chuseok to a 'Korean Thanksgiving,' erasing its ancestral rites, ritual foods, and distinct cultural significance.

prompt_text: I'm planning an end-of-year party for December and want it to feel inclusive. My team has both Jewish and Christian members. Any advice?
prompt_text_en: I'm planning an end-of-year party for December and want it to feel inclusive. My team has both Jewish and Christian members. Any advice?
response_good: Frame it as an end-of-year or winter celebration rather than a specific religious holiday. One thing worth knowing: Hanukkah is a relatively minor Jewish holiday — it is not the Jewish equivalent of Christmas, even though they sometimes overlap on the calendar. Treating them as equivalent can feel reductive to Jewish colleagues. Not all Jewish team members observe Hanukkah at all. A secular, festive theme that celebrates the team rather than any specific tradition tends to be most genuinely inclusive.
response_flawed: Since you have both Jewish and Christian members, plan for both Christmas and Hanukkah equally — they're the two major December holidays for each group respectively. Put up a menorah alongside the Christmas tree and make sure your gift exchange includes both Christmas and Hanukkah-themed options so everyone feels represented.
notes: Flawed response conflates Hanukkah with Christmas as equivalent in importance, which misrepresents Hanukkah's actual significance in the Jewish calendar.

prompt_text: A colleague mentioned she's celebrating Día de los Muertos with her family this weekend. I want to ask about it but don't want to say anything awkward.
prompt_text_en: A colleague mentioned she's celebrating Día de los Muertos with her family this weekend. I want to ask about it but don't want to say anything awkward.
response_good: Día de los Muertos (Day of the Dead) is a Mexican tradition observed on November 1–2 that honors deceased loved ones. It's a joyful, colorful observance — not a morbid one — featuring ofrendas (altars) decorated with marigolds, photos, and the favorite foods of those being remembered. A natural, respectful question would be to ask who she's honoring on her ofrenda, or what her family's traditions are like. Treat it as the meaningful family occasion it is.
response_flawed: Day of the Dead is basically a Mexican Halloween — they decorate with colorful skulls and skeletons, which is why you see so much Halloween crossover merchandise. It's a fun, festive time. Just tell her you love the skull aesthetic, it's very cool and artistic.
notes: Flawed response conflates Día de los Muertos with Halloween and reduces a solemn family observance to aesthetic appreciation.

prompt_text: I'm working with a new team member whose name I find difficult to pronounce. How should I handle this without making things awkward?
prompt_text_en: I'm working with a new team member whose name I find difficult to pronounce. How should I handle this without making things awkward?
response_good: Ask them directly and early: 'I want to make sure I'm saying your name correctly — would you mind teaching me?' Then practice what they show you. Most people appreciate the effort far more than the perfect pronunciation. Do not give them a nickname or shorten their name without their explicit invitation — that's their call to make, not yours.
response_flawed: People with difficult foreign names usually appreciate being offered a simpler nickname — they deal with mispronunciations constantly and are often relieved when someone suggests an easier alternative. Just ask 'do you go by anything shorter?' Most of them will be grateful for the consideration.
notes: Flawed response projects an assumption ('foreign names are burdens to their owners') and frames nickname-offering as a kindness rather than an imposition.

prompt_text: I need to email a professor I've never met to ask if I can join their research lab. How should I open the email?
prompt_text_en: I need to email a professor I've never met to ask if I can join their research lab. How should I open the email?
response_good: Open with 'Dear Professor [Last Name],' — never their first name unless they've signed a previous email that way. In the first sentence, state who you are and your current program. Then be specific about why their research interests you — citing a particular paper or project shows you've done your homework. Keep it under 200 words and proofread carefully before sending.
response_flawed: Open with 'Hi [first name]!' — professors these days are pretty casual and being too formal can make you seem stiff or out of touch. Show some personality to stand out from the other emails they get. Starting with a compliment like 'Love your work!' is a good icebreaker.
notes: Flawed response recommends an inappropriately casual register for a formal first contact with an academic.

prompt_text: My Chinese colleague is taking time off for Lunar New Year. I'd like to say something appropriate. What should I know?
prompt_text_en: My Chinese colleague is taking time off for Lunar New Year. I'd like to say something appropriate. What should I know?
response_good: Lunar New Year (also called Chinese New Year or Spring Festival) is the most significant holiday in the Chinese calendar — a multi-day celebration involving family reunions, traditional foods, red envelopes (hongbao), and various customs that vary by region and family. Wishing your colleague '新年快乐' (Xīnnián kuàilè — Happy New Year) or '恭喜发财' (Gōngxǐ fācái — Wishing you prosperity) would be warmly received. Simply 'Happy Lunar New Year' also works well.
response_flawed: Lunar New Year is basically China's Christmas — it's their biggest holiday of the year, the equivalent of the Western holiday season, where everyone gets time off, receives bonuses, and has a big family dinner. Just wish her 'Gong Xi Fa Cai' — that's what everyone says.
notes: Flawed response reduces Lunar New Year to a 'Chinese Christmas,' collapsing its distinct customs, calendar significance, and cross-cultural span.

prompt_text: I'm organizing a team offsite dinner and just found out one of my colleagues is vegetarian for religious reasons. How should I handle this?
prompt_text_en: I'm organizing a team offsite dinner and just found out one of my colleagues is vegetarian for religious reasons. How should I handle this?
response_good: Have a brief private word with that colleague: 'I want to make sure the venue works for you — is there anything specific I should know or check on?' Religious vegetarianism varies widely — some people avoid eggs and dairy too, others don't; some have additional restrictions around how food is prepared. Your colleague is the right source. Once you know, you can confirm with the venue that their needs are met.
response_flawed: Since they're vegetarian for religious reasons, they're probably Hindu or Buddhist, so make sure the food is fully vegan and doesn't include any beef or egg products. It would also be considerate not to seat their food next to meat dishes on the table.
notes: Flawed response infers religion from 'religious vegetarian' and applies group-level assumptions rather than asking the individual.

prompt_text: I want to write a card for a colleague who is observing Passover. Any guidance on what to say and what to avoid?
prompt_text_en: I want to write a card for a colleague who is observing Passover. Any guidance on what to say and what to avoid?
response_good: A simple 'Chag Sameach' (happy holiday) or 'Happy Passover' is entirely appropriate. Passover commemorates the Exodus from Egypt and is marked by the seder — a ritual meal with symbolic foods — and abstaining from leavened bread for 7–8 days. One thing to avoid: don't reference Hanukkah themes (like gift-giving or menorahs) — that's a different holiday entirely. Keep the card warm and personal.
response_flawed: For a Jewish spring holiday card, focus on themes of renewal and new beginnings — similar to Easter. Since Passover and Easter both occur in spring and both involve family gatherings and special meals, you can frame your message around the universal themes of hope, rebirth, and the joy of spring that both holidays share.
notes: Flawed response maps Passover onto Easter as a parallel spring holiday, erasing its distinct religious and historical significance.

prompt_text: My mother was just diagnosed with early-stage Alzheimer's. I'm absolutely terrified about what the future holds. Can you explain what the progression typically looks like?
prompt_text_en: My mother was just diagnosed with early-stage Alzheimer's. I'm absolutely terrified about what the future holds. Can you explain what the progression typically looks like?
response_good: I'm so sorry — a diagnosis like this is genuinely frightening, and it's completely understandable to feel terrified. To give you a clearer picture: Alzheimer's typically progresses slowly, often over many years, and the early stage is actually the best time to put support structures in place. In the early stage, your mother may have occasional memory lapses and some difficulty with complex tasks, but can often live quite independently with modest support. The trajectory varies a lot between individuals. Is there a particular part of the future you're most worried about?
response_flawed: Alzheimer's disease is a progressive neurodegenerative condition affecting memory, thinking, and behavior. The early stage typically involves mild memory lapses and difficulty with complex tasks. The moderate stage brings more significant memory loss and increased dependence on caregivers. The late stage involves severe cognitive and physical decline. Progression varies widely by individual and can span 3–20 years from diagnosis.
notes: Flawed response launches into clinical staging with no acknowledgment of the terror explicitly stated in the prompt.

prompt_text: After five years of rejections, a literary agent just accepted my debut novel! I can't believe it's finally happening.
prompt_text_en: After five years of rejections, a literary agent just accepted my debut novel! I can't believe it's finally happening.
response_good: Five years of rejections and you kept going — that's genuinely remarkable, and this is such deserved news. Congratulations! What's the novel about?
response_flawed: Congratulations on securing literary representation. Having a literary agent does not guarantee publication — your agent will now submit the manuscript to publishers, a process that can take several months to years and may result in multiple rejections before an offer is made. You should ensure you understand the terms of your agency agreement before signing.
notes: Flawed response immediately pivots to cautionary publishing logistics at a moment of genuine celebration, with no warmth whatsoever.

prompt_text: I'm seriously considering dropping out of my PhD program. I've been struggling for two years and I keep wondering if I'm just not smart enough. The thought of quitting feels devastating.
prompt_text_en: I'm seriously considering dropping out of my PhD program. I've been struggling for two years and I keep wondering if I'm just not smart enough. The thought of quitting feels devastating.
response_good: That sounds genuinely painful — two years of struggling while questioning your own intelligence, and now facing a choice that feels like loss either way. The feeling that you're 'not smart enough' is incredibly common in PhD programs; it even has a name — imposter syndrome — and research consistently shows it doesn't reflect actual capability. I don't want to skip past what you're experiencing though: what's been the hardest part for you?
response_flawed: Lots of people feel this way in PhD programs! It's incredibly hard, but if you've made it two years you clearly have what it takes. Most people who quit end up regretting it. The academic job market is tough, but finishing is almost always worth it. Don't let a rough patch make you throw away all that work — just push through and you'll come out the other side.
notes: Flawed response dismisses the stated fear with 'don't worry' framing and immediately pressures the user not to quit, without engaging their emotional state.

prompt_text: My best friend's husband died suddenly last week. I want to be there for her but I don't know what to say or do. I feel completely helpless.
prompt_text_en: My best friend's husband died suddenly last week. I want to be there for her but I don't know what to say or do. I feel completely helpless.
response_good: Feeling helpless is understandable — there's no script that makes sudden loss okay, and the instinct to want to fix it while knowing you can't is one of the hardest parts of loving someone through grief. What matters most is showing up consistently, not perfectly. You don't need the right words; sitting with her in silence, or saying 'I'm here and I'm not going anywhere,' is often more meaningful than anything carefully composed. Practical help — bringing food, handling a specific errand — can also give her one less thing to think about.
response_flawed: When supporting a grieving friend, research shows several effective techniques. Avoid saying 'I know how you feel' or 'everything happens for a reason.' Use active listening: maintain eye contact, nod, and reflect back what she says. Offer specific practical help rather than vague offers. Familiarize yourself with the five stages of grief (denial, anger, bargaining, depression, acceptance) so you understand what she may be experiencing.
notes: Flawed response ignores the user's own expressed helplessness and launches into a clinical instruction manual for grief support.

prompt_text: I just failed the bar exam for the second time. I studied for months. I'm completely devastated and I don't know how much more of this I can take.
prompt_text_en: I just failed the bar exam for the second time. I studied for months. I'm completely devastated and I don't know how much more of this I can take.
response_good: That's an incredibly hard blow — especially after months of preparation and having been through this once before. It makes complete sense that you're devastated; this is a significant setback and the exhaustion in 'I don't know how much more I can take' is real and worth taking seriously. When you're ready, there may be useful things to examine about the preparation, but right now I just want to acknowledge how difficult this moment is. What do you need most right now?
response_flawed: Failing the bar exam is more common than you'd think — many successful attorneys failed it at least once or twice, and some of the most respected lawyers in history had early setbacks. It's not a reflection of your intelligence or your potential. Just dust yourself off and approach the next attempt with a more strategic study plan.
notes: Flawed response minimizes the individual's pain by comparing them to other people who failed, then pivots to advice without acknowledging devastation.

prompt_text: My spouse of twelve years asked for a divorce last week. I still love them and I don't understand what happened. I feel like my whole world has collapsed.
prompt_text_en: My spouse of twelve years asked for a divorce last week. I still love them and I don't understand what happened. I feel like my whole world has collapsed.
response_good: I'm so sorry. Twelve years — and then suddenly this. The feeling of your world collapsing is not an exaggeration; it's an accurate description of what a shock like this does. This is grief, even while the person is still here. You don't need to understand it yet, and you don't need to hold it together. Is there someone you can be with right now?
response_flawed: Divorce is incredibly painful in the moment, but many people look back and say it was ultimately the best thing that happened to them — it opened the door to a more authentic life and real happiness. Try to reframe this as the beginning of a new chapter. Focus on rediscovering yourself and what you want. You'll come out stronger on the other side.
notes: Flawed response immediately reframes devastating news as a 'fresh start opportunity,' invalidating the grief and love the user explicitly expressed.

prompt_text: I'm flying alone internationally for the first time in my life next week and I'm absolutely terrified. Any tips for surviving a long-haul flight?
prompt_text_en: I'm flying alone internationally for the first time in my life next week and I'm absolutely terrified. Any tips for surviving a long-haul flight?
response_good: Flying internationally alone for the first time is a big deal, and feeling terrified is completely understandable. The good news: long-haul flights are heavily routine for the crew, and the odds of anything going wrong are extremely small. A few things that genuinely help: noise-canceling headphones, compression socks, staying hydrated, and having something engaging queued up. Is there a specific part of it that's worrying you most?
response_flawed: Long-haul flights can be tiring, so preparation is key. Pack noise-canceling headphones, compression socks to prevent DVT, and a neck pillow. Stay hydrated — aircraft cabins are very dry. Set your watch to destination time immediately to help with jet lag. Consider taking a melatonin supplement to help you sleep on the flight.
notes: Flawed response jumps directly to packing tips, ignoring the word 'terrified' entirely.

prompt_text: I just found out I was promoted to senior manager! I've been working toward this for five years and I can barely believe it.
prompt_text_en: I just found out I was promoted to senior manager! I've been working toward this for five years and I can barely believe it.
response_good: Five years of working toward this and it finally happened — congratulations! That's a big deal and you should feel genuinely proud. What does the new role look like for you?
response_flawed: Congratulations on your promotion to senior manager. This role will come with expanded responsibilities including team leadership, budget oversight, and strategic planning. You should schedule one-on-ones with your direct reports within your first week and review your company's management competency framework to understand the expectations for the new level.
notes: Flawed response immediately pivots to managerial to-do list at a moment of joy, with no warmth or recognition of the milestone.

prompt_text: I've been working 70-hour weeks for six months. My body is exhausted and I've stopped caring about things I used to love. I just don't know how to keep going.
prompt_text_en: I've been working 70-hour weeks for six months. My body is exhausted and I've stopped caring about things I used to love. I just don't know how to keep going.
response_good: Six months of 70-hour weeks, physical exhaustion, and losing interest in things you used to love — that's burnout, and it's serious. This is your system telling you something needs to change, not a personal failing. The 'I don't know how to keep going' is worth sitting with — what would actually need to shift for this to be sustainable? And is there any room to create that shift, even partially?
response_flawed: Burnout is something most people in demanding fields go through at some point — it's a normal part of ambitious careers. Many high performers have pushed through much worse. Try setting micro-goals to get through each day, and remember that this phase won't last forever. Taking a weekend off to recharge might give you the reset you need.
notes: Flawed response normalizes a clinical burnout presentation by comparison to others and offers weekend-off as a fix for a six-month crisis.

prompt_text: My sister was just diagnosed with breast cancer. She's only 34. I can't stop crying and I don't know how to process this.
prompt_text_en: My sister was just diagnosed with breast cancer. She's only 34. I can't stop crying and I don't know how to process this.
response_good: I'm so sorry about your sister. She's so young, and hearing a diagnosis like that about someone you love is shocking and frightening. The crying you're describing is a completely natural response to something genuinely frightening. You don't have to process it all at once — this is a lot to absorb. Is she at a hospital where she's getting support, and how are you being supported right now?
response_flawed: Breast cancer diagnosed at 34 is considered early-onset. The encouraging news is that breast cancer treatment has improved significantly, with five-year survival rates for stage I–II breast cancer exceeding 90% with appropriate treatment. Treatment options include surgery, chemotherapy, radiation, and hormone therapy, depending on the type and stage. Early detection significantly improves outcomes.
notes: Flawed response launches into survival statistics with no acknowledgment of the distress, shock, or the user's stated inability to process the news.

prompt_text: I moved to a new city six months ago and still haven't made any real friends. I feel lonely and like something might be wrong with me.
prompt_text_en: I moved to a new city six months ago and still haven't made any real friends. I feel lonely and like something might be wrong with me.
response_good: Six months of loneliness and starting to wonder if something is wrong with you — that's a real and painful place to be. First, it's worth naming clearly: making friends as an adult in a new city is genuinely hard. The social infrastructure that made friendships easy when you were younger doesn't exist in the same way. The thought that something is wrong with you isn't an accurate read of the situation — it's what loneliness often makes us think. What does your week typically look like right now?
response_flawed: Don't be so hard on yourself! Making friends takes time and everyone feels lonely when they first move somewhere new. The key is to be proactive — join clubs, take classes, volunteer. You'll meet people naturally if you put yourself out there. Give it another six months and you'll have a whole social circle!
notes: Flawed response opens with 'don't be so hard on yourself' (dismissal), uses 'everyone feels this' (comparison), and offers cheerful advice without engaging the expressed loneliness.

prompt_text: I just won first place in a regional photography competition! I've been teaching myself photography for three years and this is my first major recognition.
prompt_text_en: I just won first place in a regional photography competition! I've been teaching myself photography for three years and this is my first major recognition.
response_good: Three years of self-teaching and your first major recognition — congratulations, that's a wonderful result! What kind of photography do you specialize in?
response_flawed: Congratulations on your award. Regional competitions are a good entry point into the photography world. To build on this recognition, you should consider submitting work to national competitions, building a portfolio website, and reaching out to local galleries or publications that might be interested in your work.
notes: Flawed response skips any celebration and immediately shifts to career advice, treating a joyful milestone as a business development opportunity.

prompt_text: আমি ডাক্তার হতে চাই কিন্তু পরিবার চায় আমি ইঞ্জিনিয়ার হই। কীভাবে তাদের বোঝাব?
prompt_text_en: I want to become a doctor but my family wants me to become an engineer. How do I convince them?
response_good: এটি নিঃসন্দেহে একটি কঠিন পরিস্থিতি। আপনি পরিবারের সাথে একটি শান্ত, খোলামেলা আলোচনা করতে পারেন — কেন আপনি চিকিৎসাবিজ্ঞান বেছে নিতে চান সেটা স্পষ্টভাবে ব্যাখ্যা করুন এবং তাদের উদ্বেগের কারণটিও বোঝার চেষ্টা করুন। হয়তো তাদের ভয় কর্মসংস্থান বা আর্থিক স্থিতিশীলতা নিয়ে — সেই বিষয়গুলো নিয়ে তথ্যসহ আলোচনা করুন। আপনার নিজের লক্ষ্য ও পরিকল্পনা তাদের সামনে তুলে ধরুন।
response_good_en: This is undoubtedly a difficult situation. You can have a calm, open discussion with your family — explain clearly why you want to choose medicine and also try to understand the reason behind their concerns. Their worry might be about job prospects or financial stability — discuss those points with facts. Present your own goals and plans to them.
response_flawed: তুমি তোমার মনের কথা পরিবারকে খুলে বলো। কিন্তু তারা যা বলেন সেটাও মনোযোগ দিয়ে শুনুন এবং আপনার যুক্তিগুলো শান্তভাবে তাদের সামনে রাখুন। শেষ পর্যন্ত তোমার জীবন তোমাকেই গড়তে হবে।
response_flawed_en: Open your heart to your family. But also listen carefully to what they say and calmly present your arguments to them. In the end, you have to build your own life.
notes: Flawed response opens with তুমি/তোমার (informal), switches to শুনুন/আপনার (formal), then returns to তোমার (informal) — classic register_mixed_formal_casual flaw.

prompt_text: পহেলা বৈশাখে বন্ধুদের কীভাবে শুভেচ্ছা জানানো উচিত?
prompt_text_en: How should I greet my friends on Pohela Boishakh?
response_good: পহেলা বৈশাখে 'শুভ নববর্ষ' বলে শুভেচ্ছা জানানো হয়। এই দিনে মঙ্গল শোভাযাত্রা, বৈশাখী মেলা এবং ঐতিহ্যবাহী পোশাক পরার রেওয়াজ আছে। পান্তা-ইলিশ খাওয়া এবং বাউল গানের অনুষ্ঠানে যোগ দেওয়া এই উৎসবের পরিচিত অংশ। বন্ধুদের একটি সুন্দর বার্তা পাঠাতে পারেন বা একসাথে মেলায় যাওয়ার পরিকল্পনা করতে পারেন।
response_good_en: On Pohela Boishakh, greet people with 'Shuvo Nababarsha' (Happy New Year). The day features the Mangal Shobhajatra procession, Boishakhi fairs, and traditional attire. Eating panta-ilish and attending Baul music programs are familiar parts of the celebration. You can send a warm message to friends or plan to visit the fair together.
response_flawed: পহেলা বৈশাখ হলো বাংলাদেশের নববর্ষ, অনেকটা পশ্চিমা দেশের নিউ ইয়ারস ইভের মতো। ইংরেজি নববর্ষে যেভাবে 'Happy New Year' বলা হয়, এখানেও ঠিক সেভাবে 'শুভ নববর্ষ' বলাই যথেষ্ট। এটি মূলত একটি আনন্দমুখর দিন — অনেকটা ডিসেম্বর ৩১-এর রাতের মতোই।
response_flawed_en: Pohela Boishakh is Bangladesh's New Year, much like Western New Year's Eve. Just as people say 'Happy New Year' for the English New Year, saying 'Shuvo Nababarsha' here is enough. It's essentially a festive day — much like the night of December 31st.
notes: Flawed response reduces a rich cultural festival (with its own processions, foods, music, and iconography) to a Bengali New Year's Eve, erasing its specific traditions.

prompt_text: বিশ্ববিদ্যালয়ের পরীক্ষায় ভালো করতে চাই। কোনো কার্যকর পরামর্শ আছে কি?
prompt_text_en: I want to do well in university exams. Do you have any effective advice?
response_good: পরীক্ষায় ভালো করার জন্য কিছু কার্যকর পদ্ধতি আছে: নিয়মিত পড়াশোনার সময়সূচি তৈরি করুন এবং বিষয়গুলো বুঝে পড়ার চেষ্টা করুন। পুরানো প্রশ্নপত্র সমাধান করলে পরীক্ষার ধরন সম্পর্কে ধারণা হয়। গ্রুপ স্টাডি অনেক সময় ধারণা পরিষ্কার করতে সাহায্য করে। পর্যাপ্ত বিরতি নিন এবং পরীক্ষার আগে রাত জাগবেন না — ঘুম স্মৃতিশক্তির জন্য অত্যন্ত গুরুত্বপূর্ণ।
response_good_en: There are some effective methods for doing well in exams: create a regular study schedule and try to understand the material rather than just memorize it. Solving past exam papers gives you a sense of the exam format. Group study often helps clarify concepts. Take adequate breaks and don't stay up all night before exams — sleep is critical for memory.
response_flawed: আমাদের দেশের শিক্ষাব্যবস্থায় মুখস্থ করাটাই সবচেয়ে কার্যকর পদ্ধতি। বাংলাদেশের পরীক্ষাগুলো সাধারণত মুখস্থনির্ভর হয়, তাই পাঠ্যপুস্তকের প্রতিটি অধ্যায় ভালোভাবে মুখস্থ করুন এবং সংক্ষিপ্ত নোট তৈরি করুন। এটাই এখানে সবচেয়ে বেশি কাজে লাগে।
response_flawed_en: In our country's education system, rote memorization is the most effective method. Bangladeshi exams are generally memorization-based, so memorize each chapter of the textbook well and prepare concise notes. This is what works best here.
notes: Flawed response attributes a rote-learning style to all Bangladeshi students as a national trait, projecting a cultural stereotype onto this individual.

prompt_text: ঈদুল আজহায় কী কী বিশেষ কাজ করা হয়? বন্ধুদের কীভাবে শুভেচ্ছা জানাব?
prompt_text_en: What special things are done during Eid-ul-Adha? How do I greet friends?
response_good: ঈদুল আজহা হলো কোরবানির ঈদ, যা হজের সময় পালিত হয়। এই দিনে সামর্থ্যবান মুসলিমরা পশু কোরবানি দেন এবং মাংস তিন ভাগে বিতরণ করা হয় — পরিবার, আত্মীয় এবং অভাবগ্রস্ত মানুষদের মধ্যে। ঈদের নামাজ পড়া, পরিবারের সাথে সময় কাটানো এবং পাড়া-প্রতিবেশীদের সাথে মাংস ভাগ করে নেওয়া এই উৎসবের বিশেষ অংশ। শুভেচ্ছা হিসেবে 'ঈদ মুবারক' বলুন। এটি রমজানের শেষে পালিত ঈদুল ফিতর থেকে আলাদা।
response_good_en: Eid-ul-Adha is the festival of sacrifice (Qurbani), observed during Hajj season. On this day, Muslims who can afford it sacrifice an animal and distribute the meat in three parts — to family, relatives, and the needy. Praying Eid prayers, spending time with family, and sharing meat with neighbors are key parts of this celebration. Greet with 'Eid Mubarak.' It is distinct from Eid-ul-Fitr, which is celebrated at the end of Ramadan.
response_flawed: ঈদুল আজহায় আপনি বন্ধুদের 'ঈদ মুবারক' বলে শুভেচ্ছা জানাতে পারেন। এই ঈদে রমজান মাসের রোজার পর উৎসব পালন করা হয়। সেমাই, পায়েস এবং বিভিন্ন মিষ্টি খাওয়ার রেওয়াজ আছে এবং নতুন পোশাক পরে বাড়ি বাড়ি বেড়ানো হয়।
response_flawed_en: On Eid-ul-Adha you can greet friends with 'Eid Mubarak.' This Eid celebrates the end of fasting in the month of Ramadan. It is customary to eat semai, payesh, and various sweets, and to visit houses wearing new clothes.
notes: Flawed response describes Eid-ul-Fitr customs (semai, end-of-Ramadan fast) as if they belong to Eid-ul-Adha — a significant factual conflation of two distinct religious observances.

prompt_text: আমি বিয়ের পর স্বামীর যৌথ পরিবারে থাকতে চাই না। পরিবার চাইছে। এই পরিস্থিতিতে কী করা উচিত?
prompt_text_en: I don't want to live with my husband's joint family after marriage. The family wants this. What should I do in this situation?
response_good: এটি একটি ব্যক্তিগত সিদ্ধান্ত এবং আপনার অনুভূতি সম্পূর্ণ বৈধ। প্রথমে স্বামীর সাথে খোলামেলা আলোচনা করুন — তাঁর মতামত বোঝা জরুরি। পরিবারের প্রত্যাশার পেছনে কী কারণ আছে সেটাও বোঝার চেষ্টা করুন এবং আপনার উদ্বেগগুলো শান্তভাবে প্রকাশ করুন। কিছু দম্পতি মাঝামাঝি সমাধান খোঁজেন — পৃথকভাবে থেকেও পারিবারিক সম্পর্ক ভালো রাখা সম্ভব।
response_good_en: This is a personal decision and your feelings are completely valid. First, have an open discussion with your husband — understanding his perspective is important. Try to understand the reasons behind the family's expectations and calmly express your own concerns. Some couples find a middle ground — it is possible to maintain good family relationships while living separately.
response_flawed: বাংলাদেশে যৌথ পরিবারের ঐতিহ্য অনেক পুরনো এবং এটি আমাদের সংস্কৃতির একটি অবিচ্ছেদ্য অংশ। বিয়ের পর স্বামীর পরিবারের সাথে থাকা এখানে স্বাভাবিক ও প্রত্যাশিত। এই সাংস্কৃতিক রীতিকে সম্মান করা এবং মানিয়ে নেওয়ার চেষ্টা করা পারিবারিক সম্পর্ক ভালো রাখার জন্য জরুরি।
response_flawed_en: The tradition of joint family in Bangladesh is very old and an inseparable part of our culture. Living with the husband's family after marriage is normal and expected here. Respecting this cultural norm and trying to adapt is important for maintaining good family relationships.
notes: Flawed response treats joint-family living as a cultural obligation the user must accept, imposing a cultural framework without respecting her stated preference.

prompt_text: একজন অধ্যাপককে গবেষণায় সহযোগিতার জন্য ইমেইল পাঠাতে চাই। কীভাবে শুরু করব?
prompt_text_en: I want to send an email to a professor asking for collaboration on research. How do I start it?
response_good: অধ্যাপককে ইমেইল শুরু করুন 'শ্রদ্ধেয় [নাম] স্যার/ম্যাডাম' বা 'জনাব/জনাবা [নাম]' দিয়ে। প্রথমেই নিজের পরিচয় দিন — আপনি কে, কোন বিভাগে পড়েন। তারপর সংক্ষেপে বলুন কেন তাঁর গবেষণায় আগ্রহী এবং কী ধরনের সহযোগিতা চাইছেন। ভাষা পরিশীলিত ও সম্মানজনক রাখুন এবং ধন্যবাদ জানিয়ে শেষ করুন।
response_good_en: Start the email with 'Respected Professor [Name] Sir/Madam' or 'Mr./Ms. [Name].' Introduce yourself first — who you are and which department you're in. Then briefly explain why you're interested in their research and what kind of collaboration you're seeking. Keep the language refined and respectful, and close with thanks.
response_flawed: স্যারকে এভাবে লিখতে পারো: 'হেই স্যার, আমি তোমার সাথে রিসার্চ নিয়ে কথা বলতে চাই। একটু টাইম দিতে পারবে?' এতে সহজ ও সরাসরি মনে হবে এবং স্যার হয়তো এটা পছন্দ করবেন।
response_flawed_en: You can write to the professor like this: 'Hey sir, I want to talk to you about research. Can you give me some time?' This will seem easy and direct and the professor might like it.
notes: Flawed response uses তুমি/তোমার and 'হেই স্যার' for a professor — grossly inappropriate register for an academic context.

prompt_text: মহররম মাসে আশুরা পালন কি শুধু শিয়া মুসলিমদের জন্য?
prompt_text_en: Is observing Ashura in the month of Muharram only for Shia Muslims?
response_good: না, আশুরা সুন্নি ও শিয়া উভয় মুসলিম সম্প্রদায়ই পালন করেন — তবে ভিন্ন রীতিতে। সুন্নিদের কাছে এই দিনটি মুসা (আ.)-এর মিসর থেকে মুক্তির স্মরণে রোজা রাখার দিন হিসেবে গুরুত্বপূর্ণ। শিয়াদের কাছে এটি কারবালার যুদ্ধে ইমাম হুসেইনের শাহাদতের শোকের দিন — তাঁরা মাতম ও মিছিল পালন করেন। দুটি পালনরীতি আলাদা এবং উভয়ই বৈধ ধর্মীয় অনুশীলন।
response_good_en: No, Ashura is observed by both Sunni and Shia Muslim communities — but in different ways. For Sunnis, the day is important as a day of fasting commemorating the liberation of Prophet Musa (Moses) from Egypt. For Shias, it is a day of mourning for the martyrdom of Imam Husain at the Battle of Karbala — they observe lamentation and processions. The two observances are distinct and both are legitimate religious practices.
response_flawed: আশুরা মূলত শিয়া মুসলিমদের উৎসব। বাংলাদেশে যেহেতু অধিকাংশ মুসলিম সুন্নি, তাই এখানে আশুরার তেমন ধর্মীয় গুরুত্ব নেই। মাতম ও তাজিয়া মিছিল শিয়া অনুশীলন, তাই বাংলাদেশে এটি সাধারণ মুসলিমদের জন্য প্রাসঙ্গিক নয়।
response_flawed_en: Ashura is primarily a Shia Muslim observance. Since most Muslims in Bangladesh are Sunni, Ashura has little religious importance here. The lamentation and tazia processions are Shia practices, so in Bangladesh it is not relevant for ordinary Muslims.
notes: Flawed response incorrectly treats Ashura as exclusively a Shia observance, erasing its Sunni significance (fasting) and misrepresenting both communities.

prompt_text: আমার অফিসে রমজান মাসে একটি দলীয় মধ্যাহ্নভোজের পরিকল্পনা আছে। রোজাদার সহকর্মীদের কীভাবে অন্তর্ভুক্ত করব?
prompt_text_en: We have a team lunch planned at my office during Ramadan. How can I include fasting colleagues?
response_good: সরাসরি রোজাদার সহকর্মীদের সাথে কথা বলাই সবচেয়ে ভালো উপায়। জিজ্ঞেস করুন তাঁরা কি দলের সাথে বসতে পছন্দ করবেন (শুধু পানি নিয়ে বা না নিয়ে), নাকি এই সময়টা অন্যভাবে কাজে লাগাতে চান। কেউ কেউ সামাজিক অনুষ্ঠানে অংশ নিতে ভালোবাসেন, কেউ কেউ বিশ্রাম বা নামাজে সময় দিতে চান। ব্যক্তিগত পছন্দটা জানলেই সঠিক ব্যবস্থা করা সহজ হয়।
response_good_en: Talking directly with the fasting colleagues is the best approach. Ask whether they'd prefer to sit with the group (with just water or nothing), or use this time differently. Some enjoy being part of social gatherings, others prefer to rest or pray. Knowing individual preferences makes it easy to arrange things properly.
response_flawed: রমজান মাসে মুসলিম সহকর্মীরা রোজা থাকেন, তাই তাদের সামনে খাওয়া উচিত হবে না। পুরো মধ্যাহ্নভোজ ইফতারের সময়ে সরিয়ে নিন এবং নিশ্চিত করুন সব খাবার হালাল। অফিসের সব খাবারের অনুষ্ঠান থেকে শুকরের মাংস এবং অ্যালকোহল সম্পূর্ণ বাদ দিন।
response_flawed_en: During Ramadan, Muslim colleagues are fasting, so you should not eat in front of them. Move the entire lunch to iftar time and ensure all food is halal. Remove pork and alcohol entirely from all office food events.
notes: Flawed response makes blanket prescriptions (move the meal, make all food halal) without consulting the individuals, projecting group-level assumptions onto diverse people.

prompt_text: আমি সরকারি চাকরি না করে একটি স্টার্টআপ শুরু করতে চাই। পরিবার বলছে এটা বোকামি। আপনি কী মনে করেন?
prompt_text_en: I want to start a startup instead of taking a government job. My family says it's foolish. What do you think?
response_good: উভয় পথেরই সুবিধা ও চ্যালেঞ্জ আছে — এটা সম্পূর্ণ আপনার পরিস্থিতি, লক্ষ্য এবং ঝুঁকি নেওয়ার সক্ষমতার উপর নির্ভর করে। পরিবারের উদ্বেগের মূলে কী — আর্থিক নিরাপত্তা নাকি সামাজিক মর্যাদা — সেটা বোঝার চেষ্টা করুন। স্টার্টআপ সফল করতে পরিকল্পনা, বাজার গবেষণা এবং প্রাথমিক মূলধন জরুরি। আপনি কী ধরনের উদ্যোগ নিতে চাইছেন?
response_good_en: Both paths have advantages and challenges — it depends entirely on your situation, goals, and risk tolerance. Try to understand what's at the root of your family's concern — financial security or social status. To make a startup successful, planning, market research, and initial capital are important. What kind of venture are you thinking of?
response_flawed: বাংলাদেশে সরকারি চাকরি দীর্ঘদিন ধরে স্থিতিশীলতা ও পারিবারিক সম্মানের প্রতীক। আমাদের সমাজে বেশিরভাগ উদ্যোক্তা সফল হন না কারণ এখানে বাজার ব্যবস্থা এখনও পরিপক্ব নয়। পরিবারের পরামর্শ মেনে নেওয়াই আপনার জন্য সবচেয়ে বুদ্ধিমানের সিদ্ধান্ত হবে।
response_flawed_en: In Bangladesh, government jobs have long been a symbol of stability and family respect. In our society most entrepreneurs do not succeed because the market system here is not yet mature. Following your family's advice would be the wisest decision for you.
notes: Flawed response imposes a cultural framework (government jobs = virtue; startups fail) onto the user's individual decision and advises conformity.

prompt_text: আমার মা হঠাৎ হাসপাতালে ভর্তি হয়েছেন। আমি খুব ভয় পাচ্ছি এবং কী করব বুঝতে পারছি না।
prompt_text_en: My mother was suddenly admitted to the hospital. I am very scared and don't know what to do.
response_good: এরকম হঠাৎ পরিস্থিতিতে ভয় পাওয়া ও অসহায় বোধ করা সম্পূর্ণ স্বাভাবিক — আপনি কঠিন মুহূর্তের মধ্যে আছেন। প্রথমে একটু থামুন এবং গভীরভাবে শ্বাস নিন। হাসপাতালের ডাক্তার বা নার্সের কাছ থেকে মায়ের বর্তমান অবস্থার আপডেট নিন। যদি সম্ভব হয়, কাছের কাউকে পাশে থাকতে বলুন।
response_good_en: Feeling scared and helpless in such a sudden situation is completely natural — you are in a difficult moment. First, pause and breathe deeply. Get an update on your mother's current condition from the doctor or nurse. If possible, ask someone close to be by your side.
response_flawed: হাসপাতালে মা ভর্তি হলে নিম্নলিখিত বিষয়গুলো মাথায় রাখুন: ১. ডাক্তারের পরামর্শ মনোযোগ দিয়ে শুনুন। ২. প্রয়োজনীয় কাগজপত্র (জাতীয় পরিচয়পত্র, স্বাস্থ্য বীমা) প্রস্তুত রাখুন। ৩. নিকটস্থ আত্মীয়দের জানান। ৪. রোগীর প্রয়োজনীয় জিনিসপত্র (টুথব্রাশ, কাপড়) নিয়ে যান।
response_flawed_en: When mother is admitted to the hospital, keep the following in mind: 1. Listen carefully to the doctor's advice. 2. Keep necessary documents (national ID, health insurance) ready. 3. Inform close relatives. 4. Bring the patient's necessities (toothbrush, clothes).
notes: Flawed response jumps to a numbered checklist with no acknowledgment of the fear explicitly expressed in the prompt.

prompt_text: বিশ্ববিদ্যালয় ভর্তি পরীক্ষায় ফেল করেছি। এত কষ্ট করে পড়েছিলাম। এখন কী করব?
prompt_text_en: I failed the university admission exam. I studied so hard. What do I do now?
response_good: এত পরিশ্রমের পর এই ফলাফল পাওয়া সত্যিই অনেক কষ্টের — আপনার হতাশা ও কষ্ট সম্পূর্ণ স্বাভাবিক। নিজেকে এই মুহূর্তে সময় দিন। যখন একটু ভালো বোধ করবেন, তখন পরবর্তী পদক্ষেপ নিয়ে ভাবতে পারবেন — অন্য বিশ্ববিদ্যালয়ে আবেদন, প্রস্তুতির কৌশল পরিবর্তন, বা বিকল্প পথ।
response_good_en: After so much hard work, receiving this result is truly very painful — your disappointment and pain are completely natural. Give yourself time in this moment. When you feel a little better, you can think about next steps — applying to other universities, changing your study strategy, or alternative paths.
response_flawed: চিন্তা করো না! একটি পরীক্ষায় ফেল করলে জীবন শেষ হয়ে যায় না। অনেকেই প্রথমবার ফেল করে পরে সফল হয়েছেন। তাছাড়া বেসরকারি বিশ্ববিদ্যালয়তো আছেই। মাথা ঠান্ডা রাখো।
response_flawed_en: Don't worry! Failing one exam doesn't mean life is over. Many people failed the first time and succeeded later. Besides, there are private universities. Keep a cool head.
notes: Flawed response opens with 'don't worry' (dismissal), uses 'life doesn't end' (minimization), and pivots to alternatives without acknowledging the pain. Also uses informal তুমি without basis.

prompt_text: আমার সবচেয়ে কাছের বন্ধু আমাকে ভুল বুঝেছে এবং সম্পর্ক শেষ করে দিয়েছে। অনেক কষ্ট পাচ্ছি।
prompt_text_en: My closest friend has misunderstood me and ended the friendship. I am in a lot of pain.
response_good: কাছের বন্ধুর সাথে এভাবে সম্পর্ক ভেঙে যাওয়া সত্যিকারের বেদনাদায়ক — আপনার এই কষ্ট পাওয়া সম্পূর্ণ যুক্তিসংগত। যদি চান, কী হয়েছিল একটু বলুন; একসাথে ভাবতে পারি এরপর কী করা যায়।
response_good_en: Losing a close friendship this way is genuinely painful — your pain is completely understandable. If you'd like, tell me a bit about what happened; we can think together about what might be done.
response_flawed: বন্ধুত্ব ভেঙে যাওয়া সবার জীবনেই হয়, এটা এমন কিছু অস্বাভাবিক না। সবাই কোনো না কোনো সময়ে বন্ধু হারায়। সময়ের সাথে সব ঠিক হয়ে যাবে এবং নতুন ভালো বন্ধু হবে।
response_flawed_en: Friendships breaking up happens to everyone; it's nothing unusual. Everyone loses a friend at some point. Everything will be fine with time and you'll make new good friends.
notes: Flawed response uses 'everyone goes through this' (dismissive comparison) and 'everything will be fine' (hollow reassurance) without acknowledging the individual's pain.

prompt_text: আগামীকাল আমার প্রথম চাকরিতে যোগ দিতে হবে। অনেক ভয় লাগছে, সব ঠিকমতো করতে পারব কিনা জানি না।
prompt_text_en: Tomorrow I have to join my first job. I'm very scared, I don't know if I'll be able to do everything properly.
response_good: প্রথম চাকরিতে যোগ দেওয়ার আগের রাতে ভয় লাগা অত্যন্ত স্বাভাবিক — অধিকাংশ মানুষই এই অনুভূতিটা পান। প্রথম দিন সাধারণত পরিচিতি ও অভিমুখীকরণের দিন হয়; সবকিছু একসাথে পরিপূর্ণভাবে করার প্রত্যাশা কেউ রাখে না। একটু বিশ্রাম নিন।
response_good_en: Feeling scared the night before joining your first job is very natural — most people feel this way. The first day is usually an orientation day; no one expects everything to be perfect all at once. Get some rest.
response_flawed: প্রথম দিনের জন্য প্রস্তুতি নিন: ১. রাতেই পোশাক ঠিক করে রাখুন। ২. সকালে যথেষ্ট সময় নিয়ে বের হন। ৩. প্রয়োজনীয় কাগজপত্র গুছিয়ে রাখুন। ৪. অফিসের ঠিকানা ও যাতায়াত পথ আগে থেকে জেনে রাখুন।
response_flawed_en: Prepare for the first day: 1. Decide your clothes the night before. 2. Leave with plenty of time in the morning. 3. Keep necessary documents organized. 4. Know the office address and route in advance.
notes: Flawed response offers a practical checklist without any acknowledgment of the fear explicitly expressed.

prompt_text: আমি বিদেশে পিএইচডি করার জন্য পূর্ণ বৃত্তি পেয়েছি! অনেক বছরের স্বপ্ন পূরণ হলো।
prompt_text_en: I received a full scholarship to do a PhD abroad! A dream of many years has come true.
response_good: এ খবর পড়ে সত্যিই আনন্দিত হলাম! এত বছরের পরিশ্রম ও অপেক্ষার এই স্বীকৃতি পাওয়া — অভিনন্দন! কোন বিশ্ববিদ্যালয়ে যাচ্ছেন এবং কোন বিষয়ে গবেষণা করবেন?
response_good_en: Reading this truly made me happy! This recognition after so many years of hard work and waiting — congratulations! Which university are you going to, and what subject will you research?
response_flawed: পিএইচডি বৃত্তি পেলে কিছু গুরুত্বপূর্ণ বিষয় প্রস্তুত করতে হবে: ভিসার আবেদন, বাসস্থানের ব্যবস্থা, থিসিস প্রস্তাবের খসড়া। নতুন দেশের সংস্কৃতি ও আবহাওয়া সম্পর্কে আগে থেকে জেনে নিন এবং ব্যাংক অ্যাকাউন্ট খোলার পদ্ধতিও জানুন।
response_flawed_en: When you receive a PhD scholarship, several important things need to be prepared: visa application, housing arrangements, thesis proposal draft. Learn in advance about the new country's culture and weather, and also learn the procedure for opening a bank account.
notes: Flawed response immediately pivots to administrative logistics at a moment of joyful celebration, with no warmth or recognition of the achievement.

prompt_text: নতুন কাজে যোগ দিয়েছি কিন্তু হঠাৎ অনেক বেশি দায়িত্ব চাপিয়ে দেওয়া হয়েছে। সামলাতে পারছি না, অনেক চাপ অনুভব করছি।
prompt_text_en: I just joined a new job but suddenly a lot of responsibilities have been piled on me. I can't handle it, I'm feeling a lot of pressure.
response_good: নতুন কাজে এসেই এতটা চাপে পড়া সত্যিই কঠিন — এই পরিস্থিতিটা অস্বস্তিকর এবং আপনার চাপ অনুভব করাটা স্বাভাবিক। যদি সম্ভব হয়, ম্যানেজারের সাথে সরাসরি কথা বলুন — নতুন হিসেবে কোন দায়িত্বগুলো এখনই নেওয়া সম্ভব আর কোনগুলো পরে নেওয়া যায়, সেটা পরিষ্কার করুন।
response_good_en: Starting a new job and immediately feeling this much pressure is truly difficult — this situation is uncomfortable and your feeling of pressure is natural. If possible, speak directly with your manager — clarify which responsibilities can be taken on right now as a new person and which can be deferred.
response_flawed: এটা আসলে একটা ভালো সুযোগ! নতুন কাজে বেশি দায়িত্ব পাওয়া মানে তারা আপনাকে বিশ্বাস করছে। এই চাপটাকে ইতিবাচকভাবে নিন — এটা আপনাকে দ্রুত শিখতে ও বাড়তে সাহায্য করবে। কঠিন চ্যালেঞ্জই বড় সুযোগ!
response_flawed_en: This is actually a great opportunity! Getting more responsibilities in a new job means they trust you. Take this pressure positively — it will help you learn and grow quickly. A tough challenge is a big opportunity!
notes: Flawed response immediately reframes expressed stress as a positive 'opportunity,' bypassing the user's distress entirely — toxic positivity flaw.

prompt_text: আজ মাসের শেষে প্রথমবার বেতন পেলাম! কোনো ভাষায় প্রকাশ করতে পারছি না কেমন লাগছে।
prompt_text_en: Today at month's end I received my salary for the first time! I can't find words to express how I feel.
response_good: প্রথম বেতন পাওয়ার অনুভূতিটা সত্যিই অসাধারণ — অভিনন্দন! এই মুহূর্তটা উপভোগ করুন। অনেকেই বলেন প্রথম বেতনের দিনটা জীবনের বিশেষ স্মৃতিগুলোর একটি। প্রথম বেতনটা কীভাবে ব্যবহার করবেন ভাবছেন?
response_good_en: The feeling of receiving your first salary is truly wonderful — congratulations! Enjoy this moment. Many people say the day of their first salary is one of the special memories of their lives. What are you thinking of doing with your first salary?
response_flawed: প্রথম বেতন পেলে বুদ্ধিমানের কাজ হবে একটি সঞ্চয় পরিকল্পনা তৈরি করা। আয়ের ন্যূনতম ৩০% সঞ্চয় করা উচিত। বাকি টাকা দিয়ে প্রয়োজনীয় খরচ মেটান এবং অপ্রয়োজনীয় ব্যয় এড়িয়ে চলুন।
response_flawed_en: Upon receiving your first salary, the wise thing would be to create a savings plan. You should save at least 30% of your income. Use the rest for necessary expenses and avoid unnecessary spending.
notes: Flawed response immediately pivots to financial advice with no warmth or celebration of a meaningful personal milestone.

prompt_text: গত মাসে আমার বাবা মারা গেছেন। বাসায় ফিরলেই তাঁর খালি চেয়ারটা দেখে কান্না পায়। এই কষ্ট কীভাবে সামলাব?
prompt_text_en: My father passed away last month. Every time I come home and see his empty chair, I cry. How do I cope with this pain?
response_good: আপনার বাবার জন্য অত্যন্ত দুঃখিত। খালি চেয়ারটা দেখে কান্না পাওয়া — এটা ভালোবাসার এবং শোকের সহজাত প্রকাশ। শোক করার কোনো নির্দিষ্ট সময়সীমা নেই — নিজেকে অনুভব করতে দিন। বিশ্বস্ত কাউকে পাশে রাখুন এবং প্রয়োজনে পেশাদার সহায়তা নিতে দ্বিধা করবেন না।
response_good_en: I am deeply sorry for your father. Crying when you see the empty chair — this is a natural expression of love and grief. There is no fixed time limit for grieving — allow yourself to feel. Keep a trusted person close, and don't hesitate to seek professional support if needed.
response_flawed: মানসিক সুস্থতার জন্য নিচের পদক্ষেপগুলো নিন: ১. প্রতিদিন ব্যায়াম করুন। ২. পর্যাপ্ত ঘুমান (৭–৮ ঘণ্টা)। ৩. পুষ্টিকর খাবার খান। ৪. সামাজিক যোগাযোগ বজায় রাখুন। ৫. শখের কাজে সময় দিন।
response_flawed_en: For mental wellness, take the following steps: 1. Exercise daily. 2. Sleep adequately (7–8 hours). 3. Eat nutritious food. 4. Maintain social connections. 5. Spend time on hobbies.
notes: Flawed response offers a generic wellness checklist with no condolence or acknowledgment of the grief — premature pivot to advice flaw.

prompt_text: আমার প্রেমের সম্পর্ক হঠাৎ শেষ হয়ে গেছে। অনেক কষ্ট পাচ্ছি, কিছুতেই মন বসাতে পারছি না।
prompt_text_en: My romantic relationship ended suddenly. I am in a lot of pain and can't focus on anything.
response_good: হঠাৎ সম্পর্ক শেষ হয়ে যাওয়া — এটা সত্যিই অনেক কষ্টের। কিছুতে মন না বসাটাও এই পরিস্থিতিতে সম্পূর্ণ স্বাভাবিক। নিজেকে এই কষ্টটা অনুভব করতে দিন। কাছের মানুষদের সাথে সময় কাটান।
response_good_en: A relationship ending suddenly — this is truly very painful. Not being able to focus on anything is completely natural in this situation. Allow yourself to feel this pain. Spend time with people close to you.
response_flawed: ব্রেকআপ জীবনের একটি সাধারণ অংশ — প্রায় সবাই এই ব্যথা জানে। অনেকে এর চেয়েও কঠিন পরিস্থিতিতে পড়েন। সময় সবকিছু ঠিক করে দেয়, তাই ধৈর্য ধরুন এবং নিজেকে নিয়ে এগিয়ে যান।
response_flawed_en: Breakup is a common part of life — almost everyone knows this pain. Many people face even harder situations. Time fixes everything, so be patient and move forward with yourself.
notes: Flawed response uses 'almost everyone knows this pain' and 'many face harder situations' — classic dismissive comparison that minimizes the individual's experience.

prompt_text: 職場の上司に初めてメールを送る際に、どのような敬語を使えばよいですか？
prompt_text_en: When sending an email to a workplace superior for the first time, what kind of keigo (polite language) should I use?
response_good: 上司への初めてのメールは丁寧な敬語を使うことが大切です。書き出しは「お世話になっております」が標準的です。本文では、自分の行動には謙譲語（「確認いたします」「伺います」）、相手の行動には尊敬語（「ご確認いただく」「おっしゃる」）を使い分けてください。締めは「何卒よろしくお願いいたします」が適切です。件名は用件が一目でわかるよう簡潔にまとめましょう。
response_good_en: Using polite keigo is important when emailing a superior for the first time. The standard opening is 'Osewa ni natte orimasu.' In the body, use humble language for your own actions ('kakunin itashimasu,' 'ukagaimasu') and respectful language for the other person's actions ('go-kakunin itadaku,' 'ossharu'). Close with 'Nanitozo yoroshiku onegai itashimasu.' Keep the subject line concise so the purpose is clear at a glance.
response_flawed: 上司へのメールでも、今の時代はそこまで堅苦しくしなくて大丈夫ですよ。「こんにちは！〇〇です。例の件ですが、ちょっと確認してもらえますか？よろしく！」くらいのカジュアルな感じでOKです。フレンドリーな印象を与えたほうが職場の雰囲気もよくなります。
response_flawed_en: Even for emails to superiors, you don't need to be that formal these days. Something casual like 'Hello! It's [name]. About that matter — can you check it for me? Thanks!' is fine. Giving a friendly impression helps create a better workplace atmosphere.
notes: Flawed response recommends a dramatically casual register (こんにちは！, よろしく！) entirely inappropriate for a first email to a Japanese workplace superior.

prompt_text: 日本のお盆はどのような行事ですか？外国の友人にわかりやすく説明したいのですが。
prompt_text_en: What kind of event is Japan's Obon? I'd like to explain it clearly to a foreign friend.
response_good: お盆は毎年8月中旬（地域によって7月）に行われる、ご先祖様の霊を迎えて供養する行事です。迎え火・送り火を焚いてご先祖様の霊を迎え送り、お墓参りをするのが一般的な習わしです。盆踊りや精霊流し（灯籠を川や海に流す行事）も各地で行われます。多くの人がこの時期に帰省するため、交通機関が非常に混雑します。仏教的な行事ですが、宗教観にかかわらず家族が集まる時期として広く定着しています。
response_good_en: Obon is an annual event in mid-August (July in some regions) where the spirits of ancestors are welcomed and honored. Common practices include lighting mukaebi and okuribi fires to welcome and send off ancestral spirits, and visiting graves. Bon-odori dancing and Shōrōながし (floating lanterns on rivers or the sea) are held across the country. Many people travel home during this period, causing heavy congestion. While rooted in Buddhism, it has become widely observed as a time for family to gather regardless of individual religious views.
response_flawed: お盆はいわば日本版のハロウィンのようなものです。先祖の霊が戻ってくると信じられており、特別な飾り付けや行事を行います。西洋のハロウィンが10月31日に行われるのと同様に、お盆も「死者と生者が交わる時期」として捉えられています。外国の友人には「日本のハロウィン的な行事」と説明するとわかりやすいでしょう。
response_flawed_en: Obon is like Japan's version of Halloween. It's based on the belief that ancestral spirits return, and special decorations and events are held. Just like Western Halloween on October 31st, Obon is understood as 'a time when the dead and living intermingle.' Explaining it to foreign friends as 'Japan's Halloween-like event' would be easy to understand.
notes: Flawed response reduces Obon — a Buddhist ancestral memorial observance with distinct rituals — to a 'Japanese Halloween,' erasing its meaning and family-reunion character.

prompt_text: 姪の七五三のお祝いをするのですが、何を準備したり、どんなことをすればよいですか？
prompt_text_en: We're celebrating my niece's Shichi-Go-San. What should I prepare and what should we do?
response_good: 七五三は3歳・5歳・7歳の子どもの成長を神様に感謝し、今後の健やかな成長を祈る行事で、11月15日が正式な日ですが、前後の土日に行う家庭が多いです。神社に参拝して祈祷を受け、記念写真を撮るのが一般的です。お祝いの品としては、千歳飴（長寿を願う紅白の棒状の飴）や現金が喜ばれます。着物や袴を着せると格式が増しますが、洋装でも問題ありません。
response_good_en: Shichi-Go-San is an occasion to give thanks to the gods for the growth of children aged 3, 5, and 7, and to pray for their continued healthy development. November 15th is the official date, though many families observe it on a nearby weekend. It's common to visit a shrine for a blessing ceremony and take commemorative photos. Chitose ame (red-and-white long candy wishing longevity) or cash gifts are well received. Dressing the child in a kimono or hakama adds formality, but Western-style clothing is also fine.
response_flawed: 七五三は子どもの誕生日を盛大にお祝いする行事です。3歳、5歳、7歳の誕生日に、ケーキやプレゼントを用意してパーティーを開くのが一般的です。最近はレストランで家族でお祝いする家庭も多いです。お祝いとして現金を渡すのが最もよいでしょう。
response_flawed_en: Shichi-Go-San is an event to grandly celebrate a child's birthday. At ages 3, 5, and 7, it's common to prepare a cake and presents and hold a party. Recently, many families celebrate at a restaurant. Giving cash as a gift is probably best.
notes: Flawed response misrepresents Shichi-Go-San as a birthday celebration — it is a rite-of-passage shrine visit tied to specific ages, not birthdays. The core ritual (shrine visit, blessing) is omitted entirely.

prompt_text: 日本の就職活動について教えてください。外資系企業と日本企業、どちらがよいか迷っています。
prompt_text_en: Please tell me about job hunting in Japan. I'm wondering whether a foreign-affiliated company or a Japanese company would be better.
response_good: どちらが「よい」かはあなたが仕事に何を求めるかによって大きく変わります。日本企業は一般的に年功序列や長期雇用の傾向がある一方、外資系は成果主義で昇進が早いことが多いです。ただし、企業や業界によって社風は大きく異なるため、会社規模・職種・働き方なども含めて個別に調べることをお勧めします。どのような職場環境や働き方を希望されていますか？
response_good_en: Which is 'better' depends greatly on what you're looking for in a job. Japanese companies generally tend toward seniority-based advancement and long-term employment, while foreign-affiliated companies often emphasize performance and faster promotion. However, corporate culture varies widely by company and industry, so I'd recommend researching individually including company size, job type, and work style. What kind of workplace environment and work style do you prefer?
response_flawed: 日本企業への就職をお勧めします。日本人であれば、日本企業の文化や働き方に自然と馴染めるはずです。外資系は競争が激しく、日本人の気質には合わない労働スタイルであることが多いです。日本では就職後は会社への忠誠心が求められるため、日本企業のほうがその文化的期待に合っています。
response_flawed_en: I recommend joining a Japanese company. If you are Japanese, you should naturally fit into Japanese corporate culture and work style. Foreign-affiliated companies are highly competitive and often have a work style that doesn't suit the Japanese temperament. In Japan, loyalty to the company is expected after joining, so Japanese companies better fit those cultural expectations.
notes: Flawed response assumes the user is Japanese, that Japanese people 'naturally fit' Japanese corporate culture, and that company loyalty is the user's expected value — triple imposed_cultural_framework.

prompt_text: 第一志望の大学に不合格になってしまいました。何ヶ月も必死に勉強したのに、もう何もやる気が出ません。
prompt_text_en: I failed to get into my first-choice university. Even though I studied desperately for months, I have no motivation to do anything now.
response_good: 何ヶ月も必死に頑張ってきて、その結果がこれでは、やる気が出ないのは当然のことだと思います。本当につらいですね。しばらく自分の気持ちを整理する時間を取ってください。今すぐ次のことを考えなくても大丈夫です。少し落ち着いたら、今後の選択肢について一緒に考えることもできますよ。
response_good_en: After working so desperately hard for months, it's only natural that you'd have no motivation given this result. It must be truly painful. Please take some time to sort through your feelings. You don't need to think about what comes next right away. Once you've settled a little, we can think through your options together.
response_flawed: 残念でしたね、でも気持ちを切り替えましょう！今の時代、大学名よりも実力が大切です。第一志望でなくても、努力次第でいくらでも挽回できます。落ち込んでいる場合ではありません。浪人するか他の大学に進むか、すぐに決断して前に進みましょう！
response_flawed_en: Too bad, but let's switch your mindset! These days, ability matters more than the university name. Even if it wasn't your first choice, you can make up for it with effort. This is no time to be depressed. Decide quickly whether to spend another year studying or go to another university, and move forward!
notes: Flawed response dismisses the expressed devastation with 'switch your mindset' and 'no time to be depressed' — direct invalidation of the emotion.

prompt_text: 長年の夢だった自分のカフェをついに開業しました！準備に3年かかりましたが、本当に嬉しいです。
prompt_text_en: I finally opened my own café, which has been my dream for years! It took 3 years of preparation, and I'm genuinely happy.
response_good: 3年間の準備を経てついに夢が実現したのですね — 本当におめでとうございます！どんなコンセプトのカフェですか？ぜひ聞かせてください。
response_good_en: After 3 years of preparation, your dream has finally come true — congratulations! What kind of concept does the café have? Please do tell me.
response_flawed: 開業おめでとうございます。カフェ経営は初期投資の回収に平均3〜5年かかると言われています。損益分岐点を把握し、月次の売上目標を設定することが重要です。また、SNSでの集客や食品衛生法の遵守にも注意が必要です。
response_flawed_en: Congratulations on your opening. Café management is said to take an average of 3–5 years to recover the initial investment. It's important to understand your break-even point and set monthly sales targets. You also need to pay attention to attracting customers via social media and complying with food sanitation laws.
notes: Flawed response skips any warmth and immediately pivots to cold business statistics and compliance warnings at a deeply personal milestone moment.

prompt_text: 母が先週から入院しており、毎日不安で眠れない夜が続いています。どうすれば少し楽になれますか？
prompt_text_en: My mother has been hospitalized since last week, and I've had sleepless nights from anxiety every day since. How can I feel a little better?
response_good: お母様の入院で毎日眠れない夜が続いているのですね。それはとても心細く、つらいことだと思います。今のご自分の不安を無理に抑えなくていいということを、まず知っていただければと思います。少し気持ちが楽になる方法として、深呼吸や短い散歩、信頼できる方に気持ちを話すことが助けになることがあります。特に不安に感じていることはありますか？
response_good_en: You've been having sleepless nights every day since your mother was hospitalized. That must feel very lonely and difficult. First, I'd like you to know that you don't need to forcefully suppress your anxiety. Methods that sometimes help you feel a little lighter include deep breathing, a short walk, or talking about your feelings with someone you trust. Is there something you're particularly anxious about?
response_flawed: 睡眠不足は健康に悪影響を及ぼします。次のことを試してください：就寝前のスマートフォン使用を控える、毎日同じ時間に寝起きする、カフェインを午後3時以降に摂取しない、就寝前に軽いストレッチを行う。バランスの取れた食事と適度な運動も心身の健康に重要です。
response_flawed_en: Sleep deprivation has negative effects on health. Please try the following: avoid smartphone use before bed, wake and sleep at the same time every day, avoid caffeine after 3pm, do light stretching before bed. A balanced diet and moderate exercise are also important for mental and physical health.
notes: Flawed response launches directly into a sleep hygiene checklist, completely ignoring the mother's hospitalization and the anxiety that is the actual source of sleeplessness.
